title: linaria odora subsp. dulcis
intro: Linaria odora subsp. dulcis, also known as sweet toadflax, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae. It is known for its sweet-scented flowers and has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its expectorant and antispasmodic effects, which help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Linaria odora subsp. dulcis include its ability to calm the nervous system, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, making it a popular remedy for anxiety and stress. Herbal preparations of sweet toadflax can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
